author: Duke Alvarez

structureNotes.verdictFirst: Opens with torque->rigidity verdict: <8Nm desk/stand, 8-10Nm stand/cockpit, 10Nm+ cockpit, 15Nm+ 8020.
structureNotes.mountingLadder: Four explicit rungs: desk clamp -> folding stand -> steel cockpit -> 8020 profile.
structureNotes.shotgrid: 4 verified mount/cockpit tiles (desk-clamp, gt-omega-apex, nlr-gt-track, sim-lab-p1x).
structureNotes.gearpicks: 6 gearpick asides, all VERIFIED images: desk-clamp, gt-omega-apex(x2 incl what-to-buy), nlr-gt-track, sim-lab-p1x, trak-racer-tr80(x2 incl what-to-buy).
structureNotes.comparisonTable: 6-row mount vs torque-ceiling vs best-for vs catch table.
structureNotes.sourcesChecked: Dated July 2 2026, real links to NLR/Sim-Lab/Trak Racer/GT Omega + rig guides.

blockers: Next Level Racing Wheel Stand 2.0 is the article's key folding-stand example (Rung 2) but has NO verified product image, so it is text-only. Source a real photo before it can become a body visual or if the folding-stand rung must be shown pictorially.
Fanatec CSL DD (5Nm) used as a text-only desk-candidate example; fanatec-csl-dd.jpg exists on disk but is NOT in verified-product-images.json, so it was deliberately not embedded.
Price ranges left loose where sources diverged (Trak Racer TR80 $499-$699; Sim-Lab P1-X Pro ~$829). Lock single figures before final certification.
notes: All 11 embedded gear images (6 distinct) plus the verified hero are confirmed against src/data/verified-product-images.json — zero unverified images embedded. Author = curator (Duke Alvarez, racing, revenueTier A). Rebuilt to gold structure: verdict-first, four-rung mounting ladder, verified shotgrid, 6 verified gearpicks, torque-threshold comparison table, forum + warning callouts (flex/creak, bolt patterns, space/weight, monitor mounting), buy order, What-to-buy stack, dated Sources Checked with real links. sourceReviewDate/updatedDate 2026-07-02. Left as refresh per run rules; certification-ready pending the price lock-ins and (optionally) a real NLR Wheel Stand 2.0 photo. Author changed from Robert Pruitt to Duke Alvarez per brief; added goldStatus/sourceReviewDate/revenueTier/contentCluster frontmatter; expanded from 4 to 5 FAQs; corrected folding-stand torque claim from ~10Nm to ~5Nm comfortable per Sim Racing Nerd review.
